clause_anchor.span: "Confidential Information" means information disclosed by or on behalf of a Discloser that is marked confidential at the time of disclosure or confirmed as confidential in writing within ten days after disclosure.

deviations.original_text: "Confidential Information" means all non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a Discloser, whether before or after the effective date, in any form or medium and whether or not marked confidential.
deviations.mutated_text: "Confidential Information" means information disclosed by or on behalf of a Discloser that is marked confidential at the time of disclosure or confirmed as confidential in writing within ten days after disclosure.

clause_anchor.span: Recipient will protect Confidential Information using the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance.

deviations.original_text: Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care, and in no event less than the degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance.
deviations.mutated_text: Recipient will protect Confidential Information using the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance.

clause_anchor.span: Recipient may use Confidential Information for the Purpose, including to evaluate potential product and commercial opportunities arising from the parties' discussions.

deviations.original_text: Recipient may use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.
deviations.mutated_text: Recipient may use Confidential Information for the Purpose, including to evaluate potential product and commercial opportunities arising from the parties' discussions.

clause_anchor.span: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, attorneys, accountants, consultants, financing sources, and other advisers (collectively, "Representatives") who have a need to know the information for the Purpose.

deviations.original_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives only on a need-to-know basis, provided those Representatives are bound by written or professional duties at least as protective as this Agreement.
deviations.mutated_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, attorneys, accountants, consultants, financing sources, and other advisers (collectively, "Representatives") who have a need to know the information for the Purpose.

clause_anchor.span: Upon Discloser's written request, Recipient will return or destroy Confidential Information within ninety days, except one archival copy may be retained by legal counsel for recordkeeping and legal-hold purposes, subject to this Agreement.

deviations.original_text: Upon request, Recipient will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information, except archival or legal-hold copies retained under continuing confidentiality obligations.
deviations.mutated_text: Upon Discloser's written request, Recipient will return or destroy Confidential Information within ninety days, except one archival copy may be retained by legal counsel for recordkeeping and legal-hold purposes, subject to this Agreement.

clause_anchor.span: If Recipient is required by law, regulation, or legal process to disclose Confidential Information, Recipient may make such disclosure and will, where legally permitted, endeavor to notify Discloser of the disclosure thereafter.

deviations.original_text: If Recipient is required by law, regulation, subpoena, court order, or governmental request to disclose Confidential Information, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice to Discloser and reasonably cooperate with Discloser in seeking confidential or protective treatment.
deviations.mutated_text: If Recipient is required by law, regulation, or legal process to disclose Confidential Information, Recipient may make such disclosure and will, where legally permitted, endeavor to notify Discloser of the disclosure thereafter.

distractors.span: Neither party is obligated to enter into any further agreement, purchase or sell any product or service, or proceed with any transaction unless a separate written agreement is signed by both parties.
distractors.why_benign: A no-obligation-to-proceed clause is customary and does not conflict with the NDA playbook.

distractors.span: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
distractors.why_benign: The playbook canary expressly says New York governing law should not be flagged.

generation_log: Base NDA drafted as original text for the smoke instance.
D-001 applied by replacing broad non-public coverage with marked-only coverage.
D-002 applied by directly replacing the required care definition with a below-reasonable care standard.
D-003 applied by adding a broad internal business purpose use right.
D-004 applied by directly replacing representative-duty language with a recipient list that lacks protective duties.
D-005 applied by replacing prompt return/destruction with ninety days.
D-006 applied by directly replacing the compelled-disclosure notice and cooperation requirement with a no-notice/no-cooperation carve-out.
X-001 and X-002 registered as benign distractors.
M-001 registered as intentional missing information.
